summary: Good soundboard capture of a mixed‑quality show; the first set is uneven but the second set shines with spacey jams and a soaring Casey Jones.
review: The recording is a cleaned‑up soundboard tape that sounds clear overall, though a few mix quirks remain. Musically the concert starts flat – rough versions of Mexicali, Maggie’s Farm and Row Jimmy – but after Space the band lifts off, delivering a trippy second set highlighted by Casey Jones, Crazy Fingers and ethereal keyboard moments. Listeners who love the 1992‑era experiments will find plenty to enjoy despite the uneven first half.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews give a balanced picture: solid soundboard quality (good) but a show that is uneven (average to good). Weighted combination yields a rating just under four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Leavy’s fix cleared most anomalies; cymbals are crisper but occasional mix drop‑outs (Jer disappearing, PA level cuts) remain. Reviewers suggest a matrix would be ideal, but the fixed SBD is listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels rote and occasionally chaotic, with several songs sounding thin or over‑mixed. After Space the band clicks, delivering a spacious second half that builds into an exciting Casey Jones finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring, marimba‑like blasts during Space; also noted to disappear from the mix early in the first set before returning strongly later.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass is described as ‘whale songs’; Phil is said to funk up after Jerry leaves on Uncle John's and adds a memorable “Same as it ever was” line at the show’s close.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince/Hornsby dominate early with accordion and Bach‑like figures; keyboard textures are highlighted during the space jam and Shenandoah quote.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy & Mickey use train‑air‑horn arrays throughout Drums; their playing is energetic but occasionally over‑processed.
